verses.latin_vulgate: Melius est nomen bonum quam divitiæ multæ ;  super argentum et aurum gratia bona.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נִבְחָ֣ר שֵׁ֭ם מֵעֹ֣שֶׁר רָ֑ב מִכֶּ֥סֶף וּ֝מִזָּהָ֗ב חֵ֣ן טֽוֹב׃
verses.douay_rheims: A good name is better than great riches: and good favour is above silver and gold.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hyvä sanoma on kalliimpi kuin suuri rikkaus, ja suosio on parempi kuin hopia ja kulta.
verses.king_james: A good name is rather to be chosen than great riches, and loving favour rather than silver and gold.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dives et pauper obviaverunt sibi :  utriusque operator est Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָשִׁ֣יר וָרָ֣שׁ נִפְגָּ֑שׁוּ עֹשֵׂ֖ה כֻלָּ֣ם יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: The rich and poor have met one another: the Lord is the maker of them both.
verses.finnish_biblia: Rikas ja köyhä tulevat toinen toistansa vastaan: ja Herra on ne kaikki tehnyt.
verses.king_james: The rich and poor meet together: the Lord is the maker of them all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Callidus vidit malum, et abscondit se ;  innocens pertransiit, et afflictus est damno.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָר֤וּם ׀ רָאָ֣ה רָעָ֣ה (ויסתר) [וְנִסְתָּ֑ר] וּ֝פְתָיִ֗ים עָבְר֥וּ וְֽנֶעֱנָֽשׁוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The prudent man saw the evil, and hid himself: the simple passed on, and suffered loss.
verses.finnish_biblia: Viisas näkee pahan, ja karttaa; taitamattomat juoksevat lävitse, ja saavat vahingon.
verses.king_james: A prudent man foreseeth the evil, and hideth himself: but the simple pass on, and are punished.

verses.latin_vulgate: Finis modestiæ timor Domini,  divitiæ, et gloria, et vita.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֵ֣קֶב עֲ֭נָוָה יִרְאַ֣ת יְהֹוָ֑ה עֹ֖שֶׁר וְכָב֣וֹד וְחַיִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: The fruit of humility is the fear of the Lord, riches and glory and life.
verses.finnish_biblia: Nöyryyden ja Herran pelvon palkka on rikkaus, kunnia ja elämä.
verses.king_james: By humility and the fear of the Lord are riches, and honour, and life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Arma et gladii in via perversi ;  custos autem animæ suæ longe recedit ab eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: צִנִּ֣ים פַּ֭חִים בְּדֶ֣רֶךְ עִקֵּ֑שׁ שׁוֹמֵ֥ר נַ֝פְשׁ֗וֹ יִרְחַ֥ק מֵהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Arms and swords are in the way of the perverse: but he that keepeth his own soul, departeth far from them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Orjantappurat ja paulat ovat väärän tiellä; vaan joka siitä taamma vetäytyy, hän varjelee henkensä.
verses.king_james: Thorns and snares are in the way of the froward: he that doth keep his soul shall be far from them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Proverbium est : adolescens juxta viam suam ;  etiam cum senuerit, non recedet ab ea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חֲנֹ֣ךְ לַ֭נַּעַר עַל־פִּ֣י דַרְכּ֑וֹ גַּ֥ם כִּי־יַ֝זְקִ֗ין לֹא־יָס֥וּר מִמֶּֽנָּה׃
verses.douay_rheims: It is a proverb: A young man according to his way, even when he is old, he will not depart from it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niinkuin sinä lapsen totutat nuoruudessa, niin ei hän siitä luovu, kuin hän vanhenee.
verses.king_james: Train up a child in the way he should go: and when he is old, he will not depart from it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dives pauperibus imperat,  et qui accipit mutuum servus est fœnerantis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָ֭שִׁיר בְּרָשִׁ֣ים יִמְשׁ֑וֹל וְעֶ֥בֶד לֹ֝וֶ֗ה לְאִ֣ישׁ מַלְוֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: The rich ruleth over the poor: and the borrower is servant to him that lendeth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Rikas vallitsee köyhiä, ja joka lainaksi ottaa, hän on lainaajan orja.
verses.king_james: The rich ruleth over the poor, and the borrower is servant to the lender.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui seminat iniquitatem metet mala,  et virga iræ suæ consummabitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זוֹרֵ֣עַ עַ֭וְלָה (יקצור) [יִקְצׇר־]אָ֑וֶן וְשֵׁ֖בֶט עֶבְרָת֣וֹ יִכְלֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that soweth iniquity, shall reap evils, and with the rod of his anger he shall be consumed.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka vääryyttä kylvää, se niittäää vaivan, ja hänen pahuutensa hukkuu vitsalla.
verses.king_james: He that soweth iniquity shall reap vanity: and the rod of his anger shall fail.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui pronus est ad misericordiam benedicetur :  de panibus enim suis dedit pauperi.  Victoriam et honorem acquiret qui dat munera ;  animam autem aufert accipientium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: טֽוֹב־עַ֭יִן ה֣וּא יְבֹרָ֑ךְ כִּֽי־נָתַ֖ן מִלַּחְמ֣וֹ לַדָּֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that is inclined to mercy, shall be blessed: for of his bread he hath given to the poor. He that maketh presents, shall purchase victory and honour: but he carrieth away the souls of the receivers.
verses.finnish_biblia: Laupiaat silmät siunataan, sillä hän antaa leivästä köyhälle.
verses.king_james: He that hath a bountiful eye shall be blessed; for he giveth of his bread to the poor.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ejice derisorem, et exibit cum eo jurgium,  cessabuntque causæ et contumeliæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גָּ֣רֵֽשׁ לֵ֭ץ וְיֵצֵ֣א מָד֑וֹן וְ֝יִשְׁבֹּ֗ת דִּ֣ין וְקָלֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: Cast out the scoffer, and contention shall go out with him, and quarrels and reproaches shall cease.
verses.finnish_biblia: Aja pilkkaaja pois,niin riita asettuu, ja tora ja häväistys lakkaa.
verses.king_james: Cast out the scorner, and contention shall go out; yea, strife and reproach shall cease.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui diligit cordis munditiam,  propter gratiam labiorum suorum habebit amicum regem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֹהֵ֥ב (טהור) [טְהׇר־]לֵ֑ב חֵ֥ן שְׂ֝פָתָ֗יו רֵעֵ֥הוּ מֶֽלֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that loveth cleanness of heart, for the grace of his lips shall have the king for his friend.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka sydämen puhtautta rakastaa, sen huulet ovat otolliset, ja kuningas on hänen ystävänsä.
verses.king_james: He that loveth pureness of heart, for the grace of his lips the king shall be his friend.

verses.latin_vulgate: Oculi Domini custodiunt scientiam,  et supplantantur verba iniqui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֵינֵ֣י יְ֭הֹוָה נָ֣צְרוּ דָ֑עַת וַ֝יְסַלֵּ֗ף דִּבְרֵ֥י בֹגֵֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: The eyes of the Lord preserve knowledge: and the words of the unjust are overthrown.
verses.finnish_biblia: Herran silmät varjelevat hyvän neuvon, ja kukistavat ylönkatsojan sanat.
verses.king_james: The eyes of the Lord preserve knowledge, and he overthroweth the words of the transgressor.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit piger : Leo est foris ;  in medio platearum occidendus sum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָמַ֣ר עָ֭צֵל אֲרִ֣י בַח֑וּץ בְּת֥וֹךְ רְ֝חֹב֗וֹת אֵרָצֵֽחַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The slothful man saith: There is a lion without, I shall be slain in the midst of the streets.
verses.finnish_biblia: Laiska sanoo: jalopeura on ulkona, ja minä tapetaan kadulla.
verses.king_james: The slothful man saith, There is a lion without, I shall be slain in the streets.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fovea profunda os alienæ :  cui iratus est Dominus, incidet in eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שׁוּחָ֣ה עֲ֭מֻקָּה פִּ֣י זָר֑וֹת זְע֥וּם יְ֝הֹוָ֗ה (יפול) [יִפׇּל־]שָֽׁם׃
verses.douay_rheims: The mouth of a strange woman is a deep pit: he whom the Lord is angry with, shall fall into it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Porttoin suu on syvä kuoppa: joka ei Herran suosiossa ole, hän lankee siihen.
verses.king_james: The mouth of strange women is a deep pit: he that is abhorred of the Lord shall fall therein.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stultitia colligata est in corde pueri,  et virga disciplinæ fugabit eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִ֭וֶּלֶת קְשׁוּרָ֣ה בְלֶב־נָ֑עַר שֵׁ֥בֶט מ֝וּסָ֗ר יַרְחִיקֶ֥נָּה מִמֶּֽנּוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Folly is bound up in the heart of a child, and the rod of correction shall drive it away.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hulluus riippuu nuorukaisen sydämessä, vaan kurituksen vitsa ajaa sen kauvas hänestä.
verses.king_james: Foolishness is bound in the heart of a child; but the rod of correction shall drive it far from him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui calumniatur pauperem ut augeat divitias suas,  dabit ipse ditiori, et egebit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֹ֣שֵֽׁק דָּ֭ל לְהַרְבּ֣וֹת ל֑וֹ נֹתֵ֥ן לְ֝עָשִׁ֗יר אַךְ־לְמַחְסֽוֹר׃
verses.douay_rheims: He that oppresseth the poor, to increase his own riches, shall himself give to one that is richer, and shall be in need.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka köyhälle tekee vääryyttä enentääksensä tavaraansa, hänen pitää itse rikkaalle antaman, ja tosin köyhäksi tuleman.
verses.king_james: He that oppresseth the poor to increase his riches, and he that giveth to the rich, shall surely come to want.

verses.latin_vulgate: Inclina aurem tuam, et audi verba sapientium :  appone autem cor ad doctrinam meam,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַ֥ט אׇזְנְךָ֗ וּ֭שְׁמַע דִּבְרֵ֣י חֲכָמִ֑ים וְ֝לִבְּךָ֗ תָּשִׁ֥ית לְדַעְתִּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Incline thy ear, and hear the words of the wise: and apply thy heart to my doctrine:
verses.finnish_biblia: Kallista korvas ja kuule viisasten sanat, ja pane minun oppini sydämees.
verses.king_james: Bow down thine ear, and hear the words of the wise, and apply thine heart unto my knowledge.

verses.latin_vulgate: quæ pulchra erit tibi cum servaveris eam in ventre tuo,  et redundabit in labiis tuis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־נָ֭עִים כִּֽי־תִשְׁמְרֵ֣ם בְּבִטְנֶ֑ךָ יִכֹּ֥נוּ יַ֝חְדָּ֗ו עַל־שְׂפָתֶֽיךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Which shall be beautiful for thee, if thou keep it in thy bowels, and it shall flow in thy lips:
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä se on sinulle suloinen, jos sinä pidät sen mielessäs, ja ne sovitetaan yhteen sinun huulilles.
verses.king_james: For it is a pleasant thing if thou keep them within thee; they shall withal be fitted in thy lips.

verses.latin_vulgate: ut sit in Domino fiducia tua,  unde et ostendi eam tibi hodie.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לִֽהְי֣וֹת בַּ֭יהֹוָה מִבְטַחֶ֑ךָ הוֹדַעְתִּ֖יךָ הַיּ֣וֹם אַף־אָֽתָּה׃
verses.douay_rheims: That thy trust may be in the Lord, wherefore I have also shewn it to thee this day.
verses.finnish_biblia: Että sinun toivos olis Herrassa, olen minä sinua tänäpänä neuvonut; niin ota myös sinä näistä vaari.
verses.king_james: That thy trust may be in the Lord, I have made known to thee this day, even to thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce descripsi eam tibi tripliciter,  in cogitationibus et scientia :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲלֹ֤א כָתַ֣בְתִּֽי לְ֭ךָ (שלשום) [שָׁלִישִׁ֑ים] בְּמֹ֖עֵצ֣וֹת וָדָֽעַת׃
verses.douay_rheims: Behold I have described it to thee three manner of ways, in thoughts and knowledge:
verses.finnish_biblia: Enkö minä ole suurista asioista kirjoittanut sinun etees, neuvoin ja opettain?
verses.king_james: Have not I written to thee excellent things in counsels and knowledge,

verses.latin_vulgate: ut ostenderem tibi firmitatem et eloquia veritatis,  respondere ex his illis qui miserunt te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְהֽוֹדִיעֲךָ֗ קֹ֭שְׁטְ אִמְרֵ֣י אֱמֶ֑ת לְהָשִׁ֥יב אֲמָרִ֥ים אֱ֝מֶ֗ת לְשֹׁלְחֶֽיךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: That I might shew thee the certainty, and the words of truth, to answer out of these to them that sent thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Osoittaakseni sinulle vahvan totuuden perustuksen, vastatakses niitä oikein, jotka sinun lähettävät.
verses.king_james: That I might make thee know the certainty of the words of truth; that thou mightest answer the words of truth to them that send unto thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Non facias violentiam pauperi quia pauper est,  neque conteras egenum in porta :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַֽל־תִּגְזׇל־דָּ֭ל כִּ֣י דַל־ה֑וּא וְאַל־תְּדַכֵּ֖א עָנִ֣י בַשָּֽׁעַר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Do no violence to the poor, because he is poor: and do not oppress the needy in the gate:
verses.finnish_biblia: Älä ryöstä köyhää, vaikka hän köyhä on, ja älä solvaise vaivaista portissa.
verses.king_james: Rob not the poor, because he is poor: neither oppress the afflicted in the gate:

verses.latin_vulgate: quia judicabit Dominus causam ejus,  et configet eos qui confixerunt animam ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־יְ֭הֹוָה יָרִ֣יב רִיבָ֑ם וְקָבַ֖ע אֶת־קֹבְעֵיהֶ֣ם נָֽפֶשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Because the Lord will judge his cause: and will afflict them that have afflicted his soul.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä Herra ajaa heidän asiansa, ja sortaa heidän soortajansa.
verses.king_james: For the Lord will plead their cause, and spoil the soul of those that spoiled them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Noli esse amicus homini iracundo,  neque ambules cum viro furioso :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תִּ֭תְרַע אֶת־בַּ֣עַל אָ֑ף וְאֶת־אִ֥ישׁ חֵ֝מ֗וֹת לֹ֣א תָבֽוֹא׃
verses.douay_rheims: Be not a friend to an angry man, and do not walk with a furious man:
verses.finnish_biblia: Älä antaudu vihaisen miehen seuraan, ja älä ole julman tykönä,
verses.king_james: Make no friendship with an angry man; and with a furious man thou shalt not go:

verses.latin_vulgate: ne forte discas semitas ejus,  et sumas scandalum animæ tuæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פֶּן־תֶּאֱלַ֥ף אֹרְחֹתָ֑ו וְלָקַחְתָּ֖ מוֹקֵ֣שׁ לְנַפְשֶֽׁךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Lest perhaps thou learn his ways, and take scandal to thy soul.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ettes oppisi hänen teitänsä, ja saisi sielulles paulaa.
verses.king_james: Lest thou learn his ways, and get a snare to thy soul.

verses.latin_vulgate: Noli esse cum his qui defigunt manus suas,  et qui vades se offerunt pro debitis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תְּהִ֥י בְתֹֽקְעֵי־כָ֑ף בַּ֝עֹרְבִ֗ים מַשָּׁאֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: Be not with them that fasten down their hands, and that offer themselves sureties for debts:
verses.finnish_biblia: Älä ole niiden tykönä, jotka kättä lyövät ja velkaa takaavat;
verses.king_james: Be not thou one of them that strike hands, or of them that are sureties for debts.

verses.latin_vulgate: si enim non habes unde restituas,  quid causæ est ut tollat operimentum de cubili tuo ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־אֵֽין־לְךָ֥ לְשַׁלֵּ֑ם לָ֥מָּה יִקַּ֥ח מִ֝שְׁכָּבְךָ֗ מִתַּחְתֶּֽיךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: For if thou have not wherewith to restore, what cause is there that he should take the covering from thy bed?
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä jos sinulla ei ole varaa maksaa, niin vuotees otetaan altas pois.
verses.king_james: If thou hast nothing to pay, why should he take away thy bed from under thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ne transgrediaris terminos antiquos,  quos posuerunt patres tui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תַּ֭סֵּג גְּב֣וּל עוֹלָ֑ם אֲשֶׁ֖ר עָשׂ֣וּ אֲבוֹתֶֽיךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Pass not beyond the ancient bounds which thy fathers have set.
verses.finnish_biblia: Älä siirrä takaperin entisiä rajoja, jotka esi-isäs tehneet ovat.
verses.king_james: Remove not the ancient landmark, which thy fathers have set.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vidisti virum velocem in opere suo ?  coram regibus stabit, nec erit ante ignobiles.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חָזִ֡יתָ אִ֤ישׁ ׀ מָ֘הִ֤יר בִּמְלַאכְתּ֗וֹ לִֽפְנֵי־מְלָכִ֥ים יִתְיַצָּ֑ב בַּל־יִ֝תְיַצֵּ֗ב לִפְנֵ֥י חֲשֻׁכִּֽים׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Hast thou seen a man swift in his work? he shall stand before kings, and shall not be before those that are obscure.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos sinä näet nopsan miehen asiassansa, sen pitää seisoman kuninkaan edessä: ei hänen pidä seisoman halpain edessä.
verses.king_james: Seest thou a man diligent in his business? he shall stand before kings; he shall not stand before mean men.
